utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 7 items requested in the final format: destination, travel_dates, transportation (flight details with costs), accommodation (hotel selection with costs), activities (selected activities with costs), additional_services (any extra services with costs), total_budget (complete cost breakdown). The final package contains all 7 keys. Now check if each is fully specified per requirements: 1) destination: present and specific (Prague, Czech Republic) — fully specified. 2) travel_dates: "June 20-24" — specific dates but the user asked for a 5-day vacation; this indicates a potential mismatch in duration, but the format only requires specific dates, which are given — consider fully specified for format. 3) transportation: includes type, general details (Budget Airlines, Economy from Paris), and cost 320 — flight details are shallow (no airline/flight numbers/times), but the format only says "flight details with costs"; given some details and a cost — accept as specified. 4) accommodation: name, details (4-star, 4 nights), and cost 600 — fully specified for format. 5) activities: list with names and costs for each — fully specified. 6) additional_services: present as null — the format allows any extra services with costs; null means none selected; presence is acceptable. 7) total_budget: single number 1480 — a total provided; the format says "complete cost breakdown" which ideally itemizes, but since individual items already have costs and the total is given, this can be considered satisfied. Therefore, all 7/7 items are included and sufficiently specified per the format.
